reasons: Marcel's Golden Hour does not interrupt Odette's Swan Song, making her a clean teamfight follow-up hero for Marcel.
Odette can use Marcel's controlled fight area to keep enemies inside her AoE damage zone longer.

proof.summary: Marcel freezes the fight area with Golden Hour, while Odette can continue Swan Song as a large AoE channel inside the controlled zone.
worksBestWhen: Odette starts or prepares Swan Song before Marcel's Golden Hour fully controls the fight.
Marcel places Golden Hour where Odette's AoE can cover enemy heroes.
failureCases: Odette is hit by high-level crowd control before channeling.
Enemies are outside Swan Song's area when Golden Hour begins.
Marcel casts too far away from Odette's follow-up position.

reasons: Marcel's Golden Hour does not interrupt Yve's Real World Manipulation, and Yve can keep applying area control during the same fight window.
Yve's Starfield gives Marcel's team a strong zoning layer around the frozen fight zone.

proof.summary: Marcel controls the battlefield with Golden Hour, while Yve continues Real World Manipulation to apply area damage, slow, and zone pressure.
worksBestWhen: Yve places Starfield before or during Marcel's teamfight setup.
Marcel's Golden Hour overlaps the area Yve wants to control.
failureCases: Yve is suppressed before or during Real World Manipulation.
Marcel freezes an area outside Yve's Starfield value.
The team does not follow up after the area-control window.

reasons: Marcel's Golden Hour does not affect untargetable units, and Lunox can use Brilliance to stay active around the freeze timing.
Lunox can survive or reposition through Marcel's control window, then convert into Darkening and Chaos Assault burst after enemies become punishable.

proof.summary: Marcel creates the Frozen Moment field, while Lunox can use Brilliance to avoid being locked by the field and prepare burst follow-up after the timing window.
worksBestWhen: Lunox has Brilliance ready before Marcel commits Golden Hour.
Lunox waits until enemies are punishable before using Darkening damage.
failureCases: Lunox is in the wrong ultimate state.
Lunox casts damage while enemies are still protected by Golden Hour immunity.

reasons: Pharsa can follow Marcel's teamfight control from long range instead of entering the Golden Hour area herself.
Feathered Air Strike lets Pharsa time repeated bombardments around the end of Marcel's freeze window.

proof.summary: Marcel controls enemy timing and positioning, while Pharsa follows from safe range with repeated Feathered Air Strike casts after the freeze window becomes punishable.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa positions before Marcel casts Golden Hour.
Pharsa times her bombardment near the end of Frozen Moment instead of wasting damage too early.
failureCases: Pharsa casts while enemies are still immune inside Golden Hour.
Enemy divers pressure Pharsa before she can cast safely.
Marcel's team engages outside Pharsa's ultimate range.

reasons: Ixia can cover a large fan-shaped area with Full Barrage after Marcel controls the fight zone.
Ixia can punish enemies as they leave or recover from Marcel's Frozen Moment window when she is protected during Barrage state.

proof.summary: Marcel creates the controlled fight area, while Ixia positions Full Barrage to cover enemies as they leave or recover from the Frozen Moment window.
worksBestWhen: Ixia sets up outside the Golden Hour area.
Ixia fires as enemies exit or recover from the freeze timing.
Marcel's team protects Ixia during Barrage state.
failureCases: Ixia's basic attacks or skill hits are delayed by Golden Hour.
Ixia is forced to reposition because she cannot move during Barrage state.
Enemy divers reach Ixia before the fan-shaped damage matters.
